_comment: EU acts by name -> CELEX, for the ⌘K resolver (lib/resolve.py). Hand-edited, and deliberately sparse: this holds only what can't be read off the official title -- `abbr`, the acronym(s) people cite by (GDPR, NIS2, …), and `label`, a genuinely-informal nickname NOT present in the title. An act whose established short title sits in the title's trailing parenthesis (GDPR '(allmän dataskyddsförordning)', DSA '(förordningen om digitala tjänster)', Rom I '(Rom I)') carries no label here -- that name is extracted from the title mechanically (a later step), so an entry may be just an abbr, or empty until then. `abbr`/`label` may each be a string or a list; both are matched case-insensitively, and an `art N`/`artikel N`/`article N` tail becomes the `#N` fragment.
